title: CC narrative review S01 panel a1

payload.notes: CC NARRATIVE review of S01 (mode=baked), STORY only. S01 is the cover panel (page P00_cover, beat=null) — its narrative job is to open the audit film: establish the exhausted researcher, the 24h deadline pressure, and seed the unsolved structured-decoding problem that ARIS will be summoned to audit. BEAT FIDELITY 5/5 — the opening is fully delivered without ARIS yet on stage (no aris command, correctly deferred to S02): a lone researcher slumped in the warm study, the cold technical window glowing with a dead failed-run (RUN FAILED / status: FAILED / invalid json structure / schema mismatch / step 1287), the deadline made literal as a DDL T-24:00:00 countdown chip, and the topic seed dllm-schema-keyword-first hanging on-screen as the unsolved thread. The warm-study vs cold-window contrast carries the emotional register (tired human vs indifferent broken machine) and the steaming mug roots the temperature/atmosphere origin. COMPOSITION (STORY) 4/5 — reading order works (researcher → dead log window → DDL chip → topic seed), but the lower-left corner is busy with overlapping log/caption text that slightly competes with the figure for first read; docked one point for that crowding. Visual identity-lock / artifact severity / baked-text legibility / content-corruption are NOT my call — left to the visual reviewers (Gemini/Codex).
